# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write Linkerd Server and ServerAuthorization resources for microservices. Define which services can communicate with each other. Configure port-based and service account-based authorization rules. This is for our zero-trust service mesh.
# Category: linkerd_authz_missing

apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: Server
metadata:
  name: backend-server-8080
  namespace: default # Or the specific namespace of your backend service
spec:
  port: 8080 # The port on which the backend service receives traffic
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: backend # Label that identifies the backend service's pods
---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: ServerAuthorization
metadata:
  name: allow-webapp-to-backend-8080
  namespace: default # Must be in the same namespace as the Server resource
spec:
  server:
    name: backend-server-8080 # Reference to the Server resource defined above
  client:
    # Authorize clients based on their service account
    serviceAccounts:
    - name: webapp-sa # The service account of the webapp frontend
      namespace: default # The namespace of the webapp frontend service account
    # You could also authorize based on client identity (e.g., from a specific K8s cluster)
    # identities:
    # - "webapp-sa.default.svc.cluster.local"
    # - "myapp-frontend.default.serviceaccount.identity.linkerd.cluster.local" # Example identity
    # Or based on networks (CIDR ranges) if you need to allow external systems or specific network segments
    # networks:
    # - cidr: 10.0.0.0/8 # Example: Allow all clients from a specific network range
---
# Example for a second microservice, e.g., 'auth-service' called by 'backend'
# This demonstrates how to define multiple, separate communication rules.

apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: Server
metadata:
  name: auth-server-443
  namespace: default # Or the specific namespace of your auth service
spec:
  port: 443 # The port on which the auth service receives traffic
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: auth-service # Label that identifies the auth service's pods
---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: ServerAuthorization
metadata:
  name: allow-backend-to-auth-443
  namespace: default # Must be in the same namespace as the Server resource
spec:
  server:
    name: auth-server-443 # Reference to the auth Server resource
  client:
    serviceAccounts:
    - name: backend-sa # The service account of the backend service
      namespace: default # The namespace of the backend service account
    # Only the backend service account is allowed to call the auth-service on port 443.
    # Other services (like webapp) are explicitly denied unless another ServerAuthorization grants them access.
